test_description: This test case evaluates the system's ability to retrieve and synthesize information from multiple, separate document sections to answer a complex question.
question: [Fill] In integrated circuit structures having front side GaN-based devices, the backside structure includes one or more _____ metal insulator metal capacitors that are connected to the GaN device layer through _____ to improve power delivery and device performance.

gold_context_sources.evidence: An integrated circuit structure includes a front side structure including a GaN-based device layer, and one or more metallization layers above the GaN-based device layer. A backside structure is below and coupled to the GaN-based layer, the backside structure including metal layers and one or more metal insulator metal capacitors. The backside structure is coupled to the GaN-based layer by through silicon vias (TSVs). To achieve high capacitance density, embodiments can include stacking layers of the high voltage MIM, using through silicon vias (TSVs) to connect with the GaN power ICs fabricated on the front side.

gold_context_sources.section_title: OBJECTIVE CLAIMS IN DESCRIPTION OF THE EMBODIMENTS
gold_context_sources.evidence: An integrated circuit structure, comprising: a front side structure comprising: a GaN-based device layer; and one or more metallization layers above the GaN-based device layer; a backside structure below and coupled to the GaN-based layer, the backside structure including metal layers and one or more alternating laterally-recessed metal insulator metal capacitors. The backside structure is coupled to the GaN-based layer by one or more through-silicon vias.

ground_truth_answer: The correct answer is [alternating laterally-recessed, through silicon vias]. Reasoning: The integrated circuit structures described include a backside structure with one or more alternating laterally-recessed metal insulator metal (MIM) capacitors. These backside capacitors are coupled to the front side GaN-based device layer through one or more through silicon vias (TSVs). This arrangement enables high capacitance density and improved power delivery without increasing front side area, as well as solving challenges related to power network resistance and layout density. The use of TSVs ensures electrical connection between the backside MIM capacitors and the GaN devices on the front side, supporting efficient power delivery and device performance.
